channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Narges Mohammadi, Iranian Nobel Laureate, Is Hospitalized
item.description: Narges Mohammadi, a prominent human rights activist, was taken to a hospital after collapsing following a heart problem in prison.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Narges Mohammadi poses in an undated photo provided by her family. The Nobel laureate has suffered from heart ailments for years, according to her family.

item.title: Israel Said to Have Helped Defend Emirates in Iran War With Iron Dome
item.description: It appeared to be the first time the missile defense system was sent to an Arab country. The Emirates bore the brunt of Iran’s retaliatory attacks on Gulf countries during the war.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Missiles being intercepted over Tel Aviv last June.

item.title: Trump ‘Not Satisfied’ With Iran’s Proposal on Ending War
item.description: The details of the latest plan were unclear, and President Trump did not specify his objections.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Leily Nikounazar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A government-organized march in Tehran on Wednesday in support of Mojtaba Khamenei, the supreme leader.

item.title: Why U.S. Oil Companies Are Not Plugging the World’s Energy Gap
item.description: American producers are under pressure from investors to keep spending in check, and they are wary of drilling more wells because they are not sure oil prices will stay high.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: J. Emilio Flores for The New York Times
item.media:description: An oil field near Bakersfield, Calif. Western oil companies are reaping the rewards of higher energy prices, but are cautious about digging new wells.

item.title: As Israel Entrenches, Frustration With Hezbollah Turns to Support
item.description: With the cease-fire fraying and Israel demolishing villages in the south, many Hezbollah supporters in Lebanon are putting aside their annoyance with the group and turning to it for protection.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum and David Guttenfelder
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: What the End of Saudi Arabia’s Big-Spending Era Means for the Kingdom and Beyond
item.description: The kingdom is pulling back from a costly and high-profile golf venture amid mounting financial concerns, raising questions about its plans.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
item.media:description: A picture of Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, in 2022.

item.title: In Packed Cafes, Iranians Yearn to Escape War’s Wreckage
item.description: Cafes and coffee shops give Iranians affordable places to talk about their hopes, fears and the cost of living as well as to seek company in uncertain times.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Arash Khamooshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Patrons at a cafe spilling out onto the sidewalk this month in Tehran.

item.title: Trump’s Plans to Boost Weapons Production Might Not Deliver for Years
item.description: While the defense industry has announced plans to make more munitions, much of that expanded production will not quickly kick in.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The administration does not yet have the funding or congressional support for the weapons it needs for the possible future wars.

item.title: Hezbollah Using Fiber-Optic Drones Against Israeli Targets
item.description: The Lebanese militant group is attacking Israeli troops with explosive drones controlled by fiber-optic cables, like those commonly used in the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner, Natan Odenheimer, Aaron Boxerman and Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An Israeli military artillery unit on the Israeli side of the border with Lebanon on Thursday.

item.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Signals Plan to Maintain Control Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei said in a statement that Iran would establish “new legal frameworks” for the Strait of Hormuz. He also said his country would retain its nuclear capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranians held photos of the country’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, at a government-organized march in Tehran on Wednesday.

item.title: How Trump’s Iran Blockade Is Complicating a High-Stakes Trip to China
item.description: If President Trump flies to China as planned in May, the primary topic will clearly be the rippling economic effects of a war that Beijing has made clear it viewed as unnecessary.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A mural in Tehran last week showing Iran controlling the Strait of Hormuz. China imports about a third of its oil and gas through the waterway.

item.title: Venice Biennale Jury Resigns Amid Tension Over Awards Ban
item.description: The jury of the world’s most important art exhibition had said it wouldn’t consider artists from countries whose leaders are accused of crimes against humanity.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: The Israel pavilion at the Venice Biennale in 2024.

item.title: Journalist Detained in Kuwait Says He Was Stripped of Citizenship
item.description: Ahmed Shihab-Eldin, a Kuwaiti-American, was held for several weeks after posting about the Iran war. Kuwait does not appear to have commented publicly on his case.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ahmed Shihab‑Eldin in Doha, Qatar, last year.

item.title: Israel Intercepts Aid Flotilla Heading to Breach Naval Blockade of Gaza
item.description: Activists said the Israeli military boarded and disabled boats carrying humanitarian assistance to the territory in international waters near Greece.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner, Lynsey Chutel and Rawan Sheikh Ahmad
item.media:description: Boats carrying activists and humanitarian aid bound for Gaza, as part of the Global Sumud Flotilla, in Barcelona, Spain, this month.

item.title: Iranians Live With Pain and Powerlessness, Beneath a Smooth Veneer
item.description: After months of upheaval, many are attempting to get on with their lives while quietly grappling with grief, economic stress and a loss of hope.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kiana Hayeri for The New York Times
item.media:description: An emotional goodbye on Iran’s border with Turkey before an Iranian family headed to Australia for work, not knowing when they would reunite with loved ones at home.

item.title: How the War in Iran is Affecting Africa’s Economy and Fuel Supply
item.description: Despite the fragile cease-fire in the Middle East, many Africans say they are bracing for tougher times ahead and making difficult decisions about the future.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon, Golden Matonga and Matthew Mpoke Bigg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A girl using a solar-powered light to study during electricity rationing in Juba, South Sudan, last month.

item.title: Jury Delivers Mixed Verdict in Case of Afghan Charged in 2021 Kabul Attack
item.description: Jurors found the man guilty of conspiracy to support the terrorist group that took credit for a horrific bombing, but deadlocked on whether he was responsible for the attack itself.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Jim Huylebroek for The New York Times
item.media:description: People being sent away from the scene of a bomb blast outside the airport in Kabul, Afghanistan, on Aug. 26, 2021.

item.title: Takeaways From Hegseth’s Testimony on Iran War and His Tenure
item.description: It was Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s first public appearance before Congress since Operation Epic Fury began in late February.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay and Megan Mineiro
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Gen. Dan Caine testified during a congressional hearing on Wednesday.

item.title: Loss of Emirates Further Weakens OPEC’s Influence
item.description: The exit of the United Arab Emirates is the most significant in a series of departures from the oil cartel in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Oil storage in Big Spring, Texas. OPEC is less powerful than it once was, in no small part because of the rise of U.S. oil production.

item.title: Gallows Humor Provides Respite From Mideast Wars
item.description: Arab digital creators on social media are turning out edgy material to cope with the conflicts and bring levity to a pain that often feels too raw.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sánchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Samer Moumneh, wearing a red headband, recording a new sketch in a cafe in Beirut, Lebanon, last week.

item.title: Britain Summons Iran’s Ambassador Over Message to Iranians in U.K.
item.description: Iran’s embassy in London posted a message on social media inviting Iranians living in Britain to register for a ‘Sacrifice for the Homeland’ campaign.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.media:description: The Iranian ambassador to Britain, Seyed Ali Mousavi, presenting his credentials to King Charles III at Buckingham Palace in June.

item.title: Treasury Issues More Sanctions on Iranian Oil Exports
item.description: The measures aim to crack down on Iran’s shadow banking system and Chinese purchases of Iranian oil.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: “Iran’s shadow banking system serves as a critical financial lifeline for its armed forces, enabling activities that disrupt global trade and fuel violence across the Middle East,” Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said in a statement.

item.title: It’s Not Just War That’s Making the U.A.E. Leave OPEC
item.description: The United Arab Emirates’ decision to leave OPEC has rocked the region, underscoring how the country, at odds with Saudi Arabia, is increasingly charting its own course.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim and Ismaeel Naar
item.category: Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke billowing from an oil facility in Fujairah, United Arab Emirates, in March.

item.title: Iraq’s Prime Minister-Designate Thrust Into U.S.-Iran Power Struggle
item.description: Ali al-Zaidi, a political unknown, has been tasked with forming a new coalition government. Now, he must grapple with growing U.S. pressure to curb Iranian influence over Iraq.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Falih Hassan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Iraqi prime minister-designate, Ali al-Zaidi, at his office in Baghdad on Tuesday. The photo was released by his office.

item.title: Luxury Yacht Linked to Russian Oligarch Passes Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The Nord, a 464-foot vessel, appears to be tied to Aleksei A. Mordashov, a Russian steel mogul who is under American and European sanctions.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel, Ivan Nechepurenko and Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Nord, anchored off Hong Kong in 2022. In that year, the U.S. State Department publicly raised concerns after Hong Kong said it would not seize the vessel.

item.title: UAE Says It Will Leave OPEC as Iran War Strains Oil Markets
item.description: The Gulf government has long complained about the group’s quotas, which officials believe unfairly limited its exports. Its departure is expected to weaken OPEC’s influence.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: The Small U.K. Agency That’s a 911 for Ships in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: At a military base outside Portsmouth, England, a 24-hour service monitors the Persian Gulf, the Red Sea and part of the Indian Ocean, responding to distress calls.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.category: United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations Centre
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Andrew Matthews/Press Association, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations Centre monitors shipping around the Arabian Peninsula, aiding vessels in trouble.

item.title: The World Needs Natural Gas Now, but the U.S. Is Exporting All It Can
item.description: Even the largest global supplier of liquefied natural gas can’t make up for the shortfall since the war in Iran cut off an important source.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The United States is building more export terminals like this one in Cameron, La., but they will take months or years to complete.

item.title: In Iran, Cheetah Sightings Offer Rare Spots of Hope Amid War
item.description: Conservationists say they have recorded several new adult and cub Asiatic cheetahs, a critically endangered subspecies found only in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A female Asiatic cheetah named Dalbar in the Pardisan Park in Tehran in 2017. The species is under constant threat of hunters, speeding cars and wild dogs.

item.title: Iraq Taps Businessman, Ali al-Zaidi, to Form New Government
item.description: After months of tensions and pressure from both the U.S. and Iran, Ali al-Zaidi, a businessman, was named as prime minister-designate.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:description: The ceremony in Baghdad on Monday at which Ali al-Zaidi was announced as prime minister-designate. The photograph was released by the president’s media office.

item.title: Iran Offers Plan to Focus on Strait of Hormuz and Delay Nuclear Talks
item.description: In its latest offer delivered on Sunday, Iran proposed opening the key waterway to shipping traffic and lifting the U.S. blockade, while postponing the thornier nuclear issue until later.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard in Tehran last week.

item.title: Hezbollah Vows to Keep Its Weapons as Lebanon Says Israeli Strikes Killed 14
item.description: Despite a cease-fire, Israel and Hezbollah have been trading attacks almost daily.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A funeral in Maaroub, southern Lebanon, on Sunday, which according to the Lebanese Health Ministry was one of the deadliest days in the country since a truce was declared.

item.title: Former Israeli Premiers Join in Bid to Oust Netanyahu in Elections
item.description: Naftali Bennett, a right-wing politician, and Yair Lapid, a centrist, will merge parties for a vote later this year.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.media:description: Naftali Bennett, left, and Yair Lapid on Sunday in Herzliya, Israel, where they said they would unite in a party to be called Yachad, Hebrew for “together.”

item.title: What to Know About the U.S.-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: Negotiations to end the war are at an impasse over Iran’s nuclear future and the Strait of Hormuz, which remains mostly shut.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A government-organized march in Tehran on Wednesday showed support for Iran’s new supreme leader.

item.title: Iran and U.S. Sink Into Awkward Limbo of ‘No War, No Peace’
item.description: Each side is betting it can last longer than the other, analysts say. But there are risks in a stalemate without a deal.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A mural in Tehran depicting Iranian missiles attacking a U.S. Navy ship.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah Trade Strikes in Lebanon, as Iran Talks Remain on Hold
item.description: Cease-fires in Lebanon and Iran are on shaky ground, with military attacks flaring and direct talks between Washington and Tehran to end their war stalled.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A mass funeral in the southern Lebanese village of Maaroub on Sunday.

item.title: Israel’s President, Putting Off Decision on Pardon for Netanyahu, Will Push for Plea Deal
item.description: President Isaac Herzog of Israel has decided not to issue a pardon to Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in his corruption case at this time, and instead will seek mediation, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel, left, with President Isaac Herzog, in Jerusalem last week.

item.title: Why Fashion Designer Elie Saab Will Never Leave Lebanon, Despite the War
item.description: The designer and his son discuss making couture during a bombing and the reason it matters.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:description: Elie Saab and Elie Saab Jr. are united in their commitment to work through war.

item.title: Families of Iranian Children Killed in School Airstrike Pen Letter to Pope
item.description: Pope Leo said he has seen the letter from the parents of more than 100 children killed in the strike, which a preliminary inquiry found resulted from a mistake by the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A memorial in Tehran for the victims of an airstrike on an elementary school in Minab, Iran, on the first day of U.S. and Israeli bombing of the country.

item.title: Trump Cancels Witkoff and Kushner’s Trip for Iran Talks, Saying, ‘We Have All the Cards.’
item.description: The last-minute scuttling of the trip to Pakistan was the latest sign of how far apart the two sides are on reaching a deal to end the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater and Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Thursday. He decided on Saturday not to have two of his top negotiators travel to Pakistan.

item.title: Lebanese Ask, ‘What Cease-Fire?’ as Israel and Hezbollah Trade Attacks in the South
item.description: The fragile truce in Lebanon was extended after talks in Washington this week but has come under threat as Israel and Hezbollah have traded attacks.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.media:description: A mass funeral in Ramadiyeh, Lebanon, on Friday, mourning those killed in recent weeks in the war with Israel.

item.title: Trump Seeks to Abolish Iran’s Nuclear Stockpile, a Problem He Helped Create
item.description: President Trump withdrew from the Obama-era nuclear accord in 2018, saying it was the worst deal ever. But Iran responded with an enrichment spree that haunts the negotiations to this day.
item.dc:creator: William J. Broad and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: In February in preparation for a possible war with the United States, Iran moved missile launchers into positions within striking distance of Israeli and American military forces.

item.title: Under Trump, Green Card Seekers Face New Scrutiny for Views on Israel
item.description: In guidance to immigration officers, the administration describes participating in pro-Palestinian protests and criticizing Israel as “overwhelmingly negative” factors.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz and Nicholas Nehamas
item.media:description: Supporters of Palestine demonstrating in New York last year. Under new guidance issued by the Trump administration, immigrants could be denied a green card for participating in pro-Palestinian protests.

item.title: Raqqa Showcases Syria’s Tumultuous Past
item.description: The city on the Euphrates, once a cradle of civilization, suffered under a succession of recent rulers. Our visit found it hoping for change.
item.dc:creator: Carlotta Gall, Hussam Hammoud and Nanna Heitmann
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
item.media:description: Dancing in Naim Square, where ISIS once held public executions, in January after Syrian government forces captured Raqqa.

item.title: On Iran’s Border, Cooking Oil Trade Is a Snapshot of the Country’s Struggles
item.description: The war has exacerbated Iran’s economic crisis, forcing many to cross the border into Turkey to buy the most basic goods.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kiana Hayeri for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranians carrying cooking oil on Wednesday at the Kapikoy border crossing between Turkey and Iran.
